description: Events become a 'nophi' (story) when they are shaped as a telling. The teller may remember them, imagine them, adapt them, or transmute them. 'sirami' (record) is made to preserve an account, while a story carries no built-in claim that its events are factual or its interpretation is correct.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for stressed voiced dental 'n', keep the airflow nasal, and round into middle-height back 'o'. Bring the lips close without sealing them for unvoiced bilabial 'ph', then spread into high-front 'i'.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Oral stories can preserve practical memory and communal history without fixing every telling word for word. The teller's source and changes can still be named.

semantic_domains.cognition: events arranged into a sequence that can be remembered, imagined, or interpreted
semantic_domains.communication: a narrative formed for telling, reading, or sharing
